    Method
    Description
    void
    setUp()
     
    void
    tearDown()
     
    void
    testCopyInt()
    Ensures that an internal element can be copied to a different file.
    void
    testCopyIntForce()
    Ensures that an internal element can be copied to a different file replacing an existing element.
    void
    testCopyIntForceInDifferentProject()
    Ensures that an internal element can be copied to a different file across projects replacing an existing element.
    void
    testCopyIntFromSnapshotToOtherFile()
    Ensures that an internal element snapshot can be copied to a different file.
    void
    testCopyIntFromSnapshotToSameFile()
    Ensures that an internal element snapshot can be copied to the mutable copy of its file with a different name.
    void
    testCopyIntInDifferentProject()
    Ensures that a top-level internal element can be copied to a different file in a different project.
    void
    testCopyIntMultiStatus()
    Ensures that a multi status exception is generated when copying internal elements.
    void
    testCopyIntNoop()
    Ensures that copying an internal element to itself is a no-op.
    void
    testCopyIntRename()
    Ensures that an internal element can be copied to a different file, and renamed.
    void
    testCopyIntRenameForce()
    Ensures that an internal element can be copied to a different file, and renamed, overwriting an existing element.
    void
    testCopyIntSameParent()
    Ensures that an internal element can be duplicated in the same file.
    void
    testCopyIntToIntInvalidChildType()
    Ensures that an internal element can not be copied to an invalid non top-level destination.
    void
    testCopyIntToSnapshot()
    Ensures that one cannot copy to a snapshot.
    void
    testCopyIntToTopInvalidChildType()
    Ensures that an internal element can not be copied to an invalid top-level destination.
    void
    testCopyIntTree()
    Ensures that an internal element can be copied to a different file, and that all its children are copied.
    void
    testCopyIntWithCollision()
    Ensures that an internal element cannot be copied to a different file replacing an existing element if no force.
    void
    testCopyIntWithPositioning()
    Ensures that an internal element can be copied to a different file with positioning.
    void
    testCopyManyIntInvalidChildType()
    Ensures that an internal element can not be copied to an invalid non top-level destination from a multiple element copy operation.
    void
    testCopyTopWithInvalidDestination()
    Ensures that a top-level internal element cannot be copied to an invalid destination.
    void
    testCopyTopWithInvalidPositioning()
    Ensures that a top-level internal element cannot be copied to a different file with an invalid sibling used for positioning.
    void
    testCopyTopWithInvalidRenamings()
    Ensures that attempting to rename with an incorrect number of renamings fails
    void
    testMoveInt()
    Ensures that an internal element can be moved to a different file.
    void
    testMoveIntForce()
    Ensures that an internal element can be moved to a different file replacing an existing element.
    void
    testMoveIntFromSnapshot()
    Ensures that an internal element snapshot cannot be moved.
    void
    testMoveIntInDifferentProject()
    Ensures that a top-level internal element can be moved to a different file in a different project.
    void
    testMoveIntMultiStatus()
    Ensures that a multi status exception is generated when moveing top-level internal elements.
    void
    testMoveIntNoop()
    Ensures that moving an internal element to itself is a no-op.
    void
    testMoveIntRename()
    Ensures that an internal element can be moved to a different file, and renamed.
    void
    testMoveIntRenameForce()
    Ensures that an internal element can be moved to a different file, and renamed, overwriting an existing element.
    void
    testMoveIntSameParent()
    Ensures that an internal element can be moved in the same file.
    void
    testMoveIntToSnapshot()
    Ensures that an internal element cannot be moved to a snapshot.
    void
    testMoveIntWithCollision()
    Ensures that an internal element cannot be moved to a different file replacing an existing element if no force.
    void
    testMoveIntWithPositioning()
    Ensures that an internal element can be moved to a different file with positioning.
    void
    testMoveManyInvalidChildType()
    Ensures that an internal element can not be moved to an invalid non top-level destination from a multiple element move operation.
    void
    testMoveToIntInvalidChildType()
    Ensures that an element which type is not allowed by a non top-level destination parent, can not be moved to this parent.
    void
    testMoveTopWithInvalidDestination()
    Ensures that a top-level internal element cannot be moved to an invalid destination.
    void
    testMoveTopWithInvalidPositioning()
    Ensures that a top-level internal element cannot be moved to a different file with an invalid sibling used for positioning.
    void
    testMoveTopWithInvalidRenamings()
    Ensures that attempting to rename with an incorrect number of renamings fails
    void
    testMoveToTopInvalidChildType()
    Ensures that an element which type is not allowed by a top-level destination parent, can not be moved to this parent.
    void
    testRenameInt()
    Ensures that an internal element can be renamed.
    void
    testRenameIntForce()
    Ensures that an internal element can be renamed, replacing an existing element.
    void
    testRenameIntNoop()
    Ensures that renaming an internal element to itself is a no-op.
    void
    testRenameIntSnapshot()
    Ensures that an internal element snapshot cannot be renamed.
    void
    testRenameIntWithCollision()
    Ensures that an internal element cannot be renamed, replacing an existing element if no force.
    void
    testRenameIntWithInvalidNames()
    Ensures that attempting to rename with an incorrect number of renamings fails
    void
    testReorderInt()
    Ensures that an internal element can be reordered.
    void
    testReorderIntSnapshot()
    Ensures that an internal element snapshot cannot be reordered.
